page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it provides 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and features a massive 260 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong compute capabilities, particularly in single-threaded operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with strong results in Geekbench multi-core, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and static web serving throughput. It features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and cryptography acceleration, which is reflected in its strong performance in image processing and physics simulations.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c4-highcpu-32 offers high compute density, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that require substantial processing power without the overhead of excess memory. It is highly suited for compute-bound tasks such as high-performance web serving, data compression, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, it delivers 16 physical cores with dedicated allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing entirely on raw CPU performance. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, strong multi-threaded decompression, and efficient static web serving. Equipped with Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, it excels at processing vector and matrix operations. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, making it an appropriate choice for software compilation, data compression, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 features 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for high-compute workloads without local storage.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with 16 physical cores and 260 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-threaded decompression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and vector math
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based inference
